text: Risk management is the process of dealing with potential problems that could harm a project or business. The first step is identifying risks, which means recognising any possible dangers or challenges. After this, you need to develop mitigation strategies. These are actions to reduce the impact or likelihood of each risk. For example, you might train staff to prevent mistakes. However, even with good planning, some risks will still occur. That is why you should also create contingency plans. These are backup plans that explain exactly what to do if a problem happens. Finally, effective risk management requires constant monitoring. You must regularly check for new risks and review your plans to ensure they still work well.
